metadata.title: GoLife Admin
metadata.description: Operational surface for GoLife AI mission quality and system health.

shell.summary: This panel watches the same product loop as mobile: capture, risks, missions, feedback, trust.

nav.title: GoLife Admin
nav.subtitle: A field board for mission quality, trust, support, and AI cost.

items.title: Missions
items.note: Ranked output quality and risk links.

items.title: Feedback
items.note: Why users accept, reject, or complete.

items.title: Incidents
items.note: Operational issues with safe summaries only.

items.title: Billing
items.note: Plan mix, storage charges, xInsightAI posture.

items.title: xInsightAI
items.note: Internal AI usage, credits, and margin.

items.title: Privacy
items.note: Requests, data map, and encrypted collections.

items.title: Audit
items.note: Metadata-only write history for admin actions.

items.title: HomeMemory
items.note: Aggregate proof and warranty health only.

items.title: Security Settings
items.note: Auth mode, session scaffold, token posture.

items.title: Login
items.note: Operator scaffold access for internal use.

shared.privateNoteRedacted: Private note kept out of admin telemetry.
shared.legacyPrivateNoteRedacted: Legacy private note redacted from admin view.

dashboard.title: Operational pulse for the personal OS
dashboard.description: Watch whether GoLife is generating useful missions, staying inside trust boundaries, and keeping AI cost under control.
dashboard.usefulMissionsNote: North star of real product utility.
dashboard.aiCostNote: Keep the margin visible while usage grows.
dashboard.safetyRateNote: Signals whether prompts are drifting into regulated or unsafe territory.
dashboard.activeKeysNote: Healthy keys currently available to the OpenRouter executor.
dashboard.disabledKeysNote: Paused or invalid keys excluded from live routing.
dashboard.snapshotAgeNote: Older snapshots increase the chance of using stale model rankings.
dashboard.briefingNote: The board is organized around the same three questions product asks every morning: are missions useful, are risks clear, and is trust intact?
dashboard.flagsNote: Operational rollouts stay visible here so mobile, gateway, and admin work from the same switchboard.

aiCosts.title: Provider cost and endpoint pressure
aiCosts.description: The fastest way to lose margin is to ignore which endpoint is expensive, slow, or frequently falling back. This board keeps that visible.
aiCosts.totalCostNote: Estimated spend across ranked missions, classification, and support endpoints.
aiCosts.trackedEndpointsNote: Operational breakdown by endpoint rather than by provider alone.
aiCosts.averageFallbackNote: High fallback can mean prompt trouble, timeout pressure, or quota issues.
aiCosts.panelNote: Classify cheaply, rank carefully, and keep feedback storage nearly free.

feedback.title: Feedback journal
feedback.description: Accepted, completed, and rejected missions are the live teaching signal for the ranking layer.
feedback.eventsNote: Stored signals available to improve future ranking.
feedback.positiveNote: Positive signals that should reinforce similar mission patterns.
feedback.rejectedNote: The clearest early warning for repetition or poor context.
feedback.panelNote: Admin keeps status and domain metadata for learning. Private note text stays out of this surface.

users.title: User state and support signals
users.description: A compact list for account health, current plan mix, and who may need export, delete, or trust follow-up.
users.weeklyActiveNote: Users who are still inside the weekly mission loop.
users.supportQueueNote: Export or delete requests that need manual action.
users.privacyRequestsNote: Open export/delete requests visible without raw personal data.
users.panelNote: The detail page combines account profile, usage footprint, mission history, and feedback journal.
users.filterQueryPlaceholder: Search by user, masked email, or display name
users.emptyBody: Adjust the search, plan, status, or locale filters and try again.
users.detailEmptyBody: Pick a row to inspect usage, privacy, and support metadata.
users.drawerSensitiveExcluded: Sensitive content is excluded from this operator surface.

userDetail.description: This dossier combines current plan, live usage footprint, mission outcomes, and the feedback that should shape ranking next.
userDetail.aiCallsNote: Total operational gateway traffic so far.
userDetail.usefulMissionsNote: Completed or accepted missions that mattered.
userDetail.fallbackRateNote: How often this user needed deterministic fallback.
userDetail.accountNote: Support flags are early warning markers for trust, monetization, or product learning.
userDetail.behaviorNote: This page keeps only safe usage, privacy, and support metadata in the operator view.
userDetail.emptyBehavior: No mission or feedback records found for this user yet.
userDetail.metadataOnlyLabel: Sensitive content is excluded from this admin view.

featureFlags.title: Feature flags
featureFlags.description: Flags keep mobile, AI gateway, and admin aligned on which behavior is live, which is in shadow mode, and which stays behind operator control.
featureFlags.panelNote: Read fallback values if the backend is offline. When the admin API is live, each toggle sends a real PATCH request.

usage.title: Capture and mission throughput
usage.description: A per-user view of how often the product is used, how often fallback is needed, and where latency is drifting.
usage.captureEventsNote: Signals whether capture is frictionless enough to become habit.
usage.missionsGeneratedNote: How many ranked plans the system has emitted.
usage.averageLatencyNote: A direct operator check on AI responsiveness.
usage.panelNote: This is the operational view to compare capture depth, fallback, and mission execution user by user.

safety.title: Safety incidents and blocked behavior
safety.description: Operators need a compact trace of blocked outputs, regulated advice attempts, and actions that required stronger confirmation.
safety.highSeverityNote: Anything high needs immediate operator review.
safety.financeIncidentsNote: Financial advice pressure remains the most sensitive regulated area.
safety.panelNote: Each event preserves who triggered it, which rule fired, and the domain that needs closer prompt or policy work.

missions.title: Ranked outputs and linked risks
missions.description: This table is the quality gate for whether the system is creating actions worth doing, not just cards worth rendering.
missions.trackedNote: Current audit slice from the operational backend.
missions.completedNote: The strongest proof of practical utility.
missions.rejectedNote: A direct signal for repetitive or low-context recommendations.
missions.panelNote: Each row keeps domains, matched risks, usefulness, and final score in one place.
missions.empty: No mission records available in this slice yet.

organizations.title: Group operations and entitlement defaults
organizations.description: Family, Team, and Enterprise operations sit here so billing, storage, and AI policy can be managed above the single-user level.
organizations.totalOrganizationsNote: Tracked org accounts in the current control plane.
organizations.totalUsersNote: Users attached to the currently filtered organizations.
organizations.totalStorageNote: Storage footprint across the filtered organizations.
organizations.emptyBody: Adjust the current filters to inspect another organization slice.
organizations.detailEmptyBody: Select an organization to inspect its current members and defaults.

plans.title: Commercial plans and entitlement limits
plans.description: Plans define seat caps, storage policy, AI credit posture, and whether BYOK is available.
plans.totalPlansNote: Commercial plan definitions visible to operations.
plans.byokPlansNote: Plans that allow customers to bring their own OpenRouter key.
plans.maxStorageNote: Largest storage allowance currently defined in the plan catalog.

openrouterByok.title: OpenRouter BYOK
openrouterByok.description: Customer-owned OpenRouter keys are managed separately from platform keys. This surface only exposes masked metadata and health state.
openrouterByok.disclaimerBody: BYOK usage is billed by OpenRouter directly. GoLife charges platform and storage only.
openrouterByok.totalKeysNote: Customer-managed keys tracked by the platform.
openrouterByok.healthyKeysNote: Keys currently available for customer-routed traffic.
openrouterByok.disabledKeysNote: Keys removed from active use by operators or health rules.

xinsightai.title: xInsightAI credits and margin
xinsightai.description: Track internally billed AI traffic separately from BYOK usage. The ledger keeps token flow, customer charge, platform cost, and debited credits visible.
xinsightai.totalCreditsNote: Only xInsightAI traffic should debit credits.
xinsightai.customerChargeNote: What the platform charged for xInsightAI traffic in this slice.
xinsightai.platformCostNote: Underlying provider cost borne by GoLife on xInsightAI traffic.
xinsightai.byokTrafficNote: Requests present in the ledger but excluded from xInsightAI credit debit.

billing.title: Plans, charges, and account posture
billing.description: This surface explains plan mix, storage charges, xInsightAI charges, and BYOK participation without pretending a real payment processor is already wired.
billing.accountsNote: Organizations currently visible to operations billing.
billing.storageRevenueNote: Estimated storage charge based on tracked org footprint.
billing.aiRevenueNote: Customer charge attributable to internally billed AI traffic.
billing.planCountNote: Plan catalog visible to billing operations.

storage.title: Storage footprint and encryption posture
storage.description: Operators need to see storage growth, billable footprint, encrypted collections, and where retention pressure is building.
storage.disclaimerBody: HomeMemory personal content is excluded from admin. Only aggregate storage metadata and encrypted collection names are shown here.
storage.cloudStorageNote: Estimated cloud-hosted share of the billable footprint.
storage.exportBundlesNote: Estimated temporary bundle volume used by privacy exports.
storage.retentionRiskNote: Organizations whose storage posture suggests retention review.

modelCatalog.title: OpenRouter model catalog
modelCatalog.description: This cache is the raw material for routing decisions. Operators should check freshness, context windows, and structured-output capability before trusting a snapshot.
modelCatalog.panelNote: The control plane filters these models before ranking them per capability.
modelCatalog.empty: No cached models are available right now.

openrouterKeys.title: OpenRouter keys and failover health
openrouterKeys.description: Track which keys are active, which ones are degraded, and whether GoLife is rotating safely when a route or quota fails.
openrouterKeys.activeKeysNote: Healthy keys available to the gateway control plane.
openrouterKeys.disabledKeysNote: Keys excluded from routing because they are paused or unsafe.
openrouterKeys.recentEventsNote: Latest success and failure records written by the gateway.
openrouterKeys.keyPoolNote: Secrets never render here. Operators only see masked labels, health, and rotation order.
openrouterKeys.rotationLogNote: This is the first place to look when a capability starts falling back too often.

routingProfiles.title: Capability profiles
routingProfiles.description: Each capability keeps its own latency, throughput, parameter, and context rules so the gateway does not route everything with one blunt policy.
routingProfiles.panelNote: These rules gate the whole OpenRouter catalog before ranking. Keep them strict enough to protect structured outputs.

routingSnapshots.title: Current top-3 model selections
routingSnapshots.description: This page shows the ranked output of the control plane after catalog filtering, telemetry weighting, and policy checks.
routingSnapshots.capabilitiesNote: Each enabled capability should expose exactly three candidates.
routingSnapshots.snapshotAgeNote: Older snapshots increase the chance of routing stale models.
routingSnapshots.selectedRowsNote: Total ranked rows stored across all capabilities.
routingSnapshots.panelNote: The gateway asks OpenRouter for these three models in this order, with provider fallback enabled.
routingSnapshots.empty: No ranked selections are available right now.

supportQueue.title: Export and delete operations
supportQueue.description: Privacy trust is operational, not theoretical. This queue keeps export and delete requests visible and actionable.
supportQueue.exportNote: Users asking for their portable data bundle.
supportQueue.deleteNote: Users requesting full account or data removal.
supportQueue.panelNote: Export requests can now generate metadata-only operational bundles, and delete requests execute a real backend purge for server-side records only.
supportQueue.metadataOnlyNote: This workflow operates on backend metadata only, not the local mobile LifeGraph.

modelSettings.title: Provider and model settings
modelSettings.description: This page keeps the main operational model choices visible so cost, fallback, and classification strategy stay explicit.
modelSettings.panelNote: Classification should stay cheap and deterministic when possible. Higher-cost reasoning belongs in ranked synthesis or weekly review.

privacy.title: Requests, encrypted collections, and retention posture
privacy.description: Operators should see privacy operations and data boundaries without exposing private content.
privacy.dataMapBody: The admin surface shows request status, encrypted collection names, and retention notes. It does not render journal, reflection, receipt, or claim content.
privacy.openRequestsNote: Pending export or delete actions waiting on operators.
privacy.encryptedNote: Collections currently protected at rest on device.
privacy.retentionNote: Operational reminders about exports and aggregate exclusions.
privacy.emptyBody: There are no export or delete requests in this slice.

security.title: Token posture, guardrails, and admin readiness
security.description: This page keeps production blocking rules visible without pretending the current scaffold is enterprise auth.
security.productionBody: Token-only access is enough for internal development but not a complete enterprise admin security model.
security.guardrailNote: Configuration posture if this environment were promoted today.
security.keyCountNote: Server-side OpenRouter keys known to operations.
security.byokNote: Customer-managed keys tracked separately from platform billing.
security.auditNote: Most recent metadata-only admin write captured by backend.
security.tokenDetail: Secrets never render in the browser. This surface only shows whether server-side config exists.
security.settingsCardBody: Login/logout and operator identity are scaffolded for internal use. Enterprise auth must be added before production exposure.

audit.title: Metadata-only write history
audit.description: Track who changed operational settings, what target changed, and the safe diff that was recorded.
audit.eventsNote: Admin write events recorded without secrets or private content.
audit.writesNote: Current page size after actor/action filters.
audit.actorsNote: Unique operator identifiers represented in the current result.
audit.detailBody: Adjust the filters or load a slice with recorded admin writes.
audit.emptyBody: No admin writes match the current filters.

homememory.title: Aggregate proof and warranty health
homememory.description: This surface reports parser volume, reminder pressure, and encryption status without showing item names, receipt text, or claim bodies.
homememory.disclaimerBody: HomeMemory admin views are aggregate-only. Personal object names, receipt text, claim drafts, serial numbers, and file references are excluded.
homememory.proofParsesNote: Parsed receipt or proof attempts recorded by telemetry.
homememory.warrantyNote: Reminder creation events tied to warranty workflows.
homememory.claimsNote: Claim draft creation count without body visibility.
homememory.fallbackNote: How often proof parsing falls back from the preferred path.
homememory.encryptionBody: Encrypted HomeMemory collections are only listed when the mobile schema exposing them has reached main.
homememory.emptyBody: Proof parsing telemetry is not available in this environment yet.

quality.title: Mission, parser, safety, and cost quality
quality.description: This board keeps product usefulness and operator risk visible so the team can tell whether GoLife is actually working.
quality.usefulnessNote: Primary signal for whether ranked output is helping users.
quality.completionNote: Recommendation-to-action conversion at the product level.
quality.rejectionNote: Early warning for repetitive or weak guidance.
quality.parserNote: HomeMemory parser quality when telemetry is available.
quality.fallbackNote: Rate of gateway or model fallback across current traffic.
quality.safetyNote: How often safety rails stepped in during the current slice.
quality.costNote: Endpoints with cost or fallback posture outside desired range.
quality.supportNote: Open privacy/support workload requiring human review.

incidents.title: Operational incidents and watch items
incidents.description: Safe summaries consolidate safety events, routing issues, and privacy backlogs without copying sensitive user data into admin.
incidents.monitoringNote: Issues being watched but not yet resolved.
incidents.highSeverityNote: Most urgent incidents in the current slice.
incidents.emptyBody: Adjust the filters or wait for new operational events.

login.title: Operator sign-in scaffold
login.description: This login flow sets an internal operator cookie for admin UI continuity. It does not replace real enterprise authentication.
login.modeBody: Admin access still relies on server-side tokens. This form only establishes an operator session label in the web UI.
login.invalidSecretBody: The operator secret did not match the configured admin web secret.
login.warningBody: Do not present this token-only scaffold as SSO, RBAC, or managed enterprise authentication.

settingsSecurity.title: Auth scaffold and token posture
settingsSecurity.description: A compact status page for auth mode, operator cookie presence, and whether the environment is close to production-safe defaults.
settingsSecurity.warningBody: Real admin authentication is still pending. Use this scaffold only for internal development and controlled operations.
settingsSecurity.authModeNote: Current admin access uses backend tokens and a UI operator cookie scaffold.
settingsSecurity.operatorNote: This cookie is for UI continuity only and must not be treated as authorization.
